title: Conflict Diagnosis and Semantic Resolution · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: Two contributors changed a shared policy in different branches. Integrate both intents with merge-base, staged conflict, semantic resolution, tests, and remote synchronization evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Conflict-Safe Feature Integration with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints.
Find merge bases and explain three-way integration from two tips and their best common ancestor.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=2, and repair limit=3 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
